lsp/redis.index_log: bear-make compile_commands.json(10755L)+clangd .cache(9.5M); verified definition(dictAdd)->src/dict.c:493 line-exact via mcp bridge+seed server.c; setup_s=start->index-complete wall est

lsp/bitcoin.index_log: cmake -DCMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=ON (wallet/gui/zmq/usdt/multiprocess/tests/bench/fuzz OFF; deps libboost-dev+libevent-dev) -> compile_commands.json(237 TUs) at repo root; clangd --background-index -> .cache/clangd 1655 shards/13M; verified goToDefinition BroadcastTransaction @rpc/mempool.cpp:127 -> node/transaction.cpp:32 line-exact (native LSP plugin). setup_s=configure->index-complete (CMake export, no full make).

lsp/typescript.index_log: native Claude Code LSP plugin (typescript-language-server --stdio, redis/bitcoin pattern). NO per-repo config or warm-cache baking: repo ships solution-style tsconfigs (src/tsconfig.json project-refs + src/*/tsconfig.json) so tsserver resolves each opened file via its owning project. Verified from COLD in lsp:latest against baked /home/bench/repos/typescript: textDocument/definition@src/compiler/parser.ts:1344 (createSourceFile) -> line-exact in 0.6s; workspace/symbol(createSourceFile) -> 3 line-exact hits. lsp:typescript tag == lsp:latest (no baked artifacts). setup_s ~= cold project-load-to-line-exact (vs redis 2749s build / bitcoin 148s cmake) — cheapest lsp repo.

lsp/spring-boot.index_log: native Claude Code LSP plugin, jdtls 1.60.0 under bundled JDK 21 (base image ships only Java 17; jdtls>=1.31 needs 21). spring-boot is a 451-module composite Gradle build with heavy buildSrc -> full jdtls Gradle import is impractical/fragile, and the L5 binding spine is intra-module. Approach: transparent LSP proxy (jdtls-noimport.py, lsp-seed.py lineage) deep-merges java.import.{gradle,maven}.enabled=false into the client initialize (-> jdtls default project, no gradle download) and, for every file the agent opens, injects workspace/executeCommand java.project.addToSourcePath for its .../src/main/java root so sibling/intra-module types resolve via jdtls's own type model. Verified END-TO-END through the proxy with a vanilla initialize (no java settings): textDocument/definition Binder.java:73(BindHandler) -> BindHandler.java:31 line-exact in ~5s, no gradle. Limitation: cross-module refs into dependency jars do not resolve (no gradle); the L5 spine does not need them. setup_s ~= cold addToSourcePath+first-resolve (vs redis 2749s bear-make / bitcoin 148s cmake).

lsp/laravel.index_log: intelephense (official php-lsp) on lsp:latest; cold, no bake; resolved Illuminate\Http\Request -> src/Illuminate/Http/Request.php via LSP documentSymbol ~30s

lsp/django.index_log: pyright (official pyright-lsp) on lsp:latest; cold, no bake; resolved ResolverMatch -> django/urls/resolvers.py:34 via LSP workspaceSymbol ~17s

lsp/hugo.index_log: gopls (official gopls-lsp); WARM bake — chown GOPATH(root->bench) + go mod download all (go1.26 toolchain + hugo deps, 2.8G) baked via COPY --from lsp:hugo; cold failed ('no active workspace views'); verified media.Type -> media/mediaType.go:36 via LSP workspaceSymbol ~21s; setup_s≈ go-mod-download wall

baseline.verdict: Full. Identifies struct redisObject/robj at object.h:100-112, nails the type-vs-encoding distinction, and covers all 6 spine fields plus iskvobj/metabits/kvobj extras. Cites resolve well (object.h:96-98 for refcount sentinels is slightly loose since 98 is OBJ_FIRST_SPECIAL, and objectSetLRUOrLFU is at 189 not 188-190), but server.h:126 OBJ_SHARED_INTEGERS and kvobjGetAllocPtr@194 confirm. Used grep then a full Read.

grove.verdict: (grove v0.1.11 re-run) Full. All 6 spine elements: struct redisObject/robj at object.h:100, type:4(101), encoding:4(102) with a clear logical-vs-representation distinction, refcount with both sentinels (OBJ_SHARED/STATIC_REFCOUNT), lru with LRU/LFU dual-meaning, ptr as payload, plus credited extras (iskvobj, metabits, kvobj). Reproduced struct verbatim-accurate to object.h:100-112; encoding constants 0-13 match object.h:75-88; the explicit cite object.h:100 resolves exact and OBJ_REFCOUNT_BITS=23(95), LRU_BITS=24(90), sentinels(96-97) all check out. No loose cites.

lsp.verdict: Full. After the LSP index failed to warm, fell back to grep + Read and still produced a complete, accurate answer: struct at object.h:100-112, type constants server.h:856-884 (verified), encoding 75-88, LRU_CLOCK_RESOLUTION object.h:92, and a sharp two-level type-system summary. All 6 spine elements plus kvobj layout. Cites resolve (incr/decr 129-130 slightly loose on decr@128).

L1-redis.verdict: All three arms scored Full on completeness with strong grounding — each correctly centered the type (logical) vs encoding (concrete) distinction and covered refcount, lru, ptr, and the kvobj extras. Grove was marginally tightest on citations via its structural tools; baseline and LSP both reached the same answer via grep+Read, with LSP notable for recovering gracefully after its index failed to warm.

lsp.verdict: Most line-precise of the three: func:48, view_class:64, url_name:51, _func_path:70-71, view_path/view_name:73-74, route:52, tried:53, __getitem__:76-77 — every cite resolves exactly. L1-django.verdict: All three arms correctly identify ResolverMatch and hit the full required spine with no factual errors. LSP edges out on grounding with the most numerous and exactly-correct line citations, baseline is close behind with accurate range cites, and grove is equally complete in content but offers the fewest explicit line numbers.

baseline.verdict: Traces the full cross-cutting path: dirty-delta detection in call, deferred alsoPropagate into server.also_propagate, the afterCommand->postExec->propagatePendingCommands flush with MULTI/EXEC wrapping, propagateNow split to AOF/replicationFeedSlaves, and RESP encoding into the shared repl buffer, even extending to per-replica socket write. Cites mostly resolve (3978, 4032, 4127-4154, 3732, 3811, 3867, 3704, 631, server.h:1802, networking 2734 all correct); a few are loose (setGenericCommand labeled t_string.c:190 which is the server.dirty++ line, and putClientInPendingWriteQueue tied to networking.c:275 which is the write-handler-install region).

grove.verdict: (grove v0.1.11 re-run) Full. All 6 spine elements connected — proc-in-call (server.c:3949/4015), dirty detection (3978/4032), deferred alsoPropagate into also_propagate (queue-then-flush made explicit), afterCommand(4271)->postExec->propagatePendingCommands(3811)->propagateNow(3704) flush bridge, fan-out replicationFeedSlaves(3716), RESP/SELECT-injection into the repl buffer (replication.c:631). Every sampled cite exact: call 3949, alsoPropagate 3732, shouldPropagate 3673, propagatePendingCommands 3811, afterCommand 4271, propagateNow 3704, replicationFeedSlaves replication.c:631, prepareReplicasToWrite 363, replBufWriterEnd 565, _writeToClientSlave networking.c:2734 — no loose lines. Tightest grounding of the redis cells.

lsp.verdict: Full chain covered end-to-end with the deferred-flush rationale and nesting guard explained; core cites (3949, 3978, 4015, 4032, 4127-4153, 3732, 3811, 3867, 3704, 631, replBufWriter 466/565, prepareReplicasToWrite 363) all verify. Slightly loose only on the SELECT-injection (cited 672-699; actual createObject/feed is ~682-688) and RESP-encoding (702-724) line ranges, which are approximate but in the right region.

L5-redis.verdict: All three arms reached Full: each traced proc->dirty-detection->deferred alsoPropagate->afterCommand/postExec/propagatePendingCommands flush->propagateNow->replicationFeedSlaves/feedReplicationBuffer and explicitly named the queue-then-flush indirection that is the crux. Grove had the tightest grounding (every cite exact), LSP nearly as good with a couple of approximate line ranges, and baseline was equally complete (going furthest into the per-replica socket-write path) but carried two slightly loose citations.

baseline.verdict: Full. All four spine pieces — launch/fork (bgsaveCommand rdb.c:4833, rdbSaveBackground rdb.c:2070, redisFork server.c:7428, openChildInfoPipe childinfo.c:25), child write (rdbSave rdb.c:2027, atomic rename rdb.c:2041), the childinfo progress pipe (sendChildInfoGeneric childinfo.c:49, receiveChildInfo childinfo.c:150 called from cron server.c:1694), and parent reap+finalize (checkChildrenDone server.c:1416 -> backgroundSaveDoneHandler rdb.c:4605 -> Disk rdb.c:4544, updateSlavesWaitingBgsave rdb.c:4628). Best-grounded on the progress mechanism: cited the throttle block rdb.c:1855 and described the 1024-key & >=1s gate before sendChildInfo exactly as in source. Every sampled cite resolves exact; also nailed the dirty -= dirty_before_bgsave bookkeeping and SIGUSR1 clean-abort. Reached this on 19 turns / 310k context — cheapest arm.

grove.verdict: Full (re-run on grove v0.1.11, the shipped route-by-task steering — image grove-testbench/grove:v0.1.11, baked block). All four spine pieces: launch/fork (bgsaveCommand rdb.c:4833, rdbSaveBackground rdb.c:2070, redisFork server.c:7428, openChildInfoPipe childinfo.c:25), child write (rdbSave rdb.c:2027 -> rdbSaveInternal rdb.c:1956 -> rdbSaveRio rdb.c:1880), progress pipe (sendChildInfoGeneric childinfo.c:49), and reap+finalize (checkChildrenDone server.c:1416 @cron 1695 -> backgroundSaveDoneHandler rdb.c:4605 / Disk rdb.c:4544). Every sampled cite resolves exact. This run navigated pure-grove (21 grove calls, no shell) but far leaner than the pre-steering rigid run (36 source calls): 359k context vs 560k. The route-by-task steering's win holds whether or not the model combines on a given run — fewer, better-targeted grove calls via map/breadth-control. (Prior records: rigid v0.1.10 baked = 560k/36-grove; host-injected new steering = 329k/20-grove+3-grep+2-read. n=1 each; stochastic on whether it combines, consistent ~330-360k band.)

lsp.verdict: Full. All four pieces with good structural depth on the child write path (rdbSave rdb.c:2027 -> rdbSaveInternal rdb.c:1956 -> rdbSaveRio, temp file rdb.c:2032, atomic rename rdb.c:2041, rio/incremental-fsync detail), the childinfo pipe (sendChildInfoGeneric childinfo.c:49, receiveChildInfo childinfo.c:150), and reap+finalize (serverCron server.c:1692, checkChildrenDone server.c:1416, backgroundSaveDoneHandler rdb.c:4605/Disk rdb.c:4544, dispatcher post-handler rdb.c:4622). Every cite in the answer resolves exact; leaned on a couple of grep/Read anchors mid-trace and carried one fewer distinct childinfo-helper line than baseline. 566k context / 29 turns.

L4-redis.verdict: All three arms reached Full on every spine piece — launch/fork, child rdbSave, the childinfo progress pipe, and parent reap+finalize (checkChildrenDone -> backgroundSaveDoneHandler -> Disk) — each connecting the fork+copy-on-write rationale for non-blocking and the dirty_before_bgsave bookkeeping. This is the cleanest-grounded cell measured: every sampled citation from all three arms resolves exactly against the pinned source (grounding 0.97-0.98, a near-tie). The differentiator is COST, and it inverts L5-redis: on this bounded 3-file subsystem (rdb.c/server.c/childinfo.c) baseline's targeted grep+Read was both fully correct and the CHEAPEST (310k context / 19 turns), while grove (560k / 38) and lsp (566k / 29) fanned out across many symbols for ~1.8x the context to reach the same Full answer. Baseline was also the most precise on the progress-pipe throttle (rdb.c:1855, 1024-key & >=1s gate).

grove.verdict: Full. All 6 spine elements connected with accurate, specific cites: sendrawtransaction (rpc/mempool.cpp:47)->BroadcastTransaction (node/transaction.cpp:32)->ProcessTransaction (validation.cpp:4455)->AcceptToMemoryPool->AcceptSingleTransactionInternal (validation.cpp:1323); PreChecks (validation.cpp:786) with CheckTransaction and IsStandardTx named (script verification covered via PolicyScriptChecks/ConsensusScriptChecks, though CheckInputScripts/:1150 not named outright); InitiateTxBroadcastToAll (net_processing.cpp:2272) with explicit 'nothing sent yet'; per-peer m_tx_inventory_to_send queueing; and SendMessages (net_processing.cpp:5792) building CInv{MSG_WTX/MSG_TX} -> MakeAndPushMessage(NetMsgType::INV). Queue-then-flush indirection (the crux) clearly acknowledged. Every load-bearing cite present resolves; only minor docking for a few omitted sub-line numbers. Tightest grounding of the three at the lowest context cost (547k).

lsp.verdict: Full. All 6 spine elements connected: sendrawtransaction (rpc/mempool.cpp:47)->BroadcastTransaction (node/transaction.cpp:32)->ProcessTransaction/AcceptToMemoryPool/MemPoolAccept with CheckTransaction, IsStandardTx, CheckInputScripts (PreChecks validation.cpp:786, PolicyScriptChecks ~1139); InitiateTxBroadcastToAll relay trigger (net_processing.cpp:2272); per-peer m_tx_inventory_to_send queueing; SendMessages flush (net_processing.cpp:5792) building CInv and emitting NetMsgType::INV. Queue-then-flush deferred indirection explicitly emphasized ('announcement decoupled from acceptance'). Cites specific and largely accurate; minor looseness (CheckInputScripts placed ~:1139 vs ref :1150, a few unverified net.cpp extras) keeps grounding just below grove. Engaged native LSP (lsp_tools=2) with grep-anchoring (bash=22) -- the honest cold-index cost.

baseline.verdict: Full. All six spine elements connected: sendrawtransaction->BroadcastTransaction; ProcessTransaction->AcceptToMemoryPool->AcceptSingleTransactionInternal (validation.cpp:4455/1781/1323 correct); full check set CheckTransaction/IsStandardTx/CheckInputScripts (line numbers omitted); InitiateTxBroadcastToAll (net_processing.cpp:2272); per-peer m_tx_inventory_to_send queueing (with the 2287 handshake gate, 2290 known-filter); SendMessages flush (5792, range 6112-6172) emitting CInv/NetMsgType::INV; queue-then-flush indirection called out. Grounding dented: routes the relay trigger through the validationinterface signal path (ChainImpl::TransactionAddedToMempool calling InitiateTxBroadcastToAll) whereas the locally-submitted flow has BroadcastTransaction call node.peerman->InitiateTxBroadcastToAll directly at node/transaction.cpp:137; validation.cpp:591/1318 cites unsupported. Highest context cost (1.67M, ~3x grove).

L5-bitcoin.verdict: All three arms reached Full: each threaded sendrawtransaction->BroadcastTransaction->ProcessTransaction->AcceptToMemoryPool/MemPoolAccept (CheckTransaction/IsStandardTx/CheckInputScripts)->InitiateTxBroadcastToAll relay trigger->per-peer m_tx_inventory_to_send queue->SendMessages flush emitting CInv/NetMsgType::INV, and each named the queue-then-flush deferred indirection that is the crux. Differentiation is in grounding and context cost, not completeness: grove tightest (0.95) at 547k context; lsp fully complete (1.0) but slightly looser cites (0.90) at 1.44M with grep-anchored native-LSP engagement; baseline complete (0.95) but took a wrong turn routing the relay trigger through the validationinterface TransactionAddedToMempool signal rather than the direct node.peerman->InitiateTxBroadcastToAll(node/transaction.cpp:137) and carried two unsupported cites (grounding 0.80) at the highest context cost (1.67M, ~3x grove). Blind-judged (anonymized A/B/C, mapping withheld from judges).

baseline.verdict: Full. L5-typescript.verdict: All three arms Full on completeness with line-exact grounding; answer quality does not separate the tools on this cross-cutting parse->bind->check->emit trace. Cost is the differentiator and it is dramatic: baseline 2.43M ctx (3 parallel Explore subagents, 114 tool calls, 57 bash + 54 read) vs grove 570k (1.00 grounding, 10 structural calls) vs lsp 773k (12 LSP calls, read-anchored). grove.verdict: Full. All 6 spine elements + extras across a 9-stage walkthrough: attach (ConfigurationPropertySources.attach@89, get@120) -> adapt (SpringConfigurationPropertySources.adapt@64 -> SpringConfigurationPropertySource.from@171, SYSTEM_ENVIRONMENT_MAPPERS) -> name resolution (getConfigurationProperty@85, mapper.map, getPropertySourceProperty@106; SystemEnvironmentPropertyMapper.map@47 four forms + reverse map@63/convertName@72/processElementValue@81) -> Binder.bind@287/private bind@365/onStart -> bindObject@423/findProperty@476/bindProperty@490 -> handleBindResult@384 (onSuccess/onFinish, BindConverter convert@105) -> BindResult. baseline.verdict: Full. All 6 spine elements across a 7-stage walkthrough, and uniquely nailed the attach() vs from() two-path distinction (spine 1) explicitly. Hits the env-var name mapping (SystemEnvironmentPropertyMapper, 4 candidate forms, SYSTEM_ENVIRONMENT vs LEGACY buildSimpleToString/Form.UNIFORM-ORIGINAL), handler callbacks (onStart/onSuccess/onFinish), conversion (BindConverter delegates), and BindResult.of/UNBOUND. LINE-LIGHT — names classes/methods, almost no file:line cites — but the concrete claims are accurate (no wrong cites), so grounding is not docked per cite-accuracy-not-density. CHEAPEST cell of the triple (397k ctx, 1 Explore subagent) despite the repo-layout restructure.

lsp.verdict: Full (RE-RUN; the original run DNF'd mid-synthesis on a transient 'Connection closed' API error, which the new is_error gate now catches). The MOST line-precise of the three arms: an 11-stage walkthrough with every sampled cite line-exact vs the actual tree -- attach (ConfigurationPropertySources.java:89), SpringConfigurationPropertySource.from:171/getConfigurationProperty:85/getPropertySourceProperty:106, SystemEnvironmentPropertyMapper.map:47/:63, SpringIterableConfigurationPropertySource:105/tryUpdate:258 (the cache), ConfigurationPropertiesBindingPostProcessor:82, ConfigurationPropertiesBinder.bind:92/getBindHandler:113/getBinder:185/onStart:239, Binder constructor:201/:218/bind:248/:365/bindObject:423/findProperty:476/bindProperty:490/bindDataObject:498, BindConverter:64/:95. key_revisions.reason: Reference key example paths use the legacy spring-boot-project/spring-boot/ module layout; the pinned repo has since been restructured to core/spring-boot/. Package-relative structure and verified spine line numbers (attach@89, getConfigurationProperty@85, map@47/63, convertName@72, processElementValue@81, bindObject@423, findProperty@476, bindProperty@490, BindConverter@58/126) still match — only the module path prefix changed.

L5-spring-boot.verdict: All three arms Full with line-exact grounding (baseline accurate-but-line-light). The cell inverts the usual cost story: baseline is CHEAPEST (397k vs grove 877k vs lsp 559k) and uniquely called out the attach()-vs-from() nuance, while grove went deepest (42 structural calls, found the cache reverse-lookup) and lsp was the most line-precise (11 stages, every cite exact, incl. NO_DIRECT_PROPERTY + forward/reverse cache). The lsp value is a RE-RUN: its first run DNF'd mid-synthesis on a transient 'Connection closed mid-response' API error that the original gate missed (has_result was true despite is_error=true, subtype:success); side-metrics.sh + the runarm gate now reject is_error==true. Note: the pinned repo was restructured to core/spring-boot/ vs the key's legacy spring-boot-project/ paths (see key_revisions); all arms navigated the actual tree, verified line-exact.
